situation: Freelancer with Blue Card (Freiberufler classification) applied to AOK Berlin for statutory health insurance. Had income from three sources: consulting clients, affiliate revenue, and occasional employment contracts. AOK could not determine primary occupation for contribution calculation.
rejection_reason: Application put on hold pending clarification of primary employment status. AOK required a formal Bescheid from Finanzamt confirming freelancer status and a prior-year income breakdown.
what_worked: Submitted the Steuerbescheid from the previous year plus a written declaration of expected annual income split by source. AOK processed membership within 1 week. Contributions were set based on self-declared estimated income with annual reconciliation.
what_failed: Submitting just the Gewerbeschein (trade license). Providing bank statements showing income deposits. Asking AOK to accept an accountant letter instead of the Finanzamt Bescheid.
